query_log.query: 2026-03-18 Reuters NY market summary dollar yen Brent WTI 10-year yield
2026-03-18 TDnet 業績予想 修正 配当 自己株 みんかぶ
2026-03-18 Reuters 商船三井 エリオット 千代田化工 レアアース 東電

snapshot: The Nikkei morning session ended at 54,898.44, up 1,198.05, while TOPIX was up roughly 1.6%, with firmer U.S. equities and calmer oil helping a broad rebound.
Reuters' NIYcm1 quote page showed Nikkei futures at 54,170 as of March 18, up 405, indicating overnight futures also recovered.
USD/JPY stayed around 159, leaving both post-BOJ yen-weakness expectations and intervention risk in the market.
Brent was at $102.27 and WTI at $94.67 in Asian trading. Resource leadership remains, but the one-way urgency seen at the open has eased.
At midday, the main focal points were Elliott-linked Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, hydrogen-equipment name Chiyoda, and guidance-and-dividend reviser Reiwa AH.

drivers.why_today: The Elliott report on Mitsui O.S.K. Lines remains the afternoon's central stock-specific theme. TEPCO also jumped on capital-alliance speculation, reinforcing the market's focus on corporate-action stories.

The joint rare-earth and copper development story lifted Mitsubishi Materials and the trading houses, while Chiyoda surged on the Toyota-linked hydrogen equipment report. The policy chain clearly broadened.

drivers.driver: After-close TDnet guidance and dividend revisions
drivers.why_today: Names such as Reiwa AH, OpenWork and tripla supplied fresh number-backed catalysts, helping individual stock-picking remain active into the afternoon.

headline_inventory.headline: U.S. equities closed higher on March 17, with the S&P 500 up 0.25% and the Nasdaq up 0.47% even as oil-related inflation worries remained
Oil futures edged lower in Asian trading, with Brent at $102.27 and WTI at $94.67 as of 10:08 JST
USD/JPY traded around 159 in the morning, with post-BOJ yen-weakness expectations and intervention risk both in play
The Nikkei morning session ended at 54,898.44, up 1,198.05, as easing oil pressure and firmer U.S. stocks drove a broad rebound
Rare-earth and copper names including Mitsubishi Materials drew buying after reports of a U.S.-Japan joint development agreement
Chiyoda surged as much as 15% on reports tied to Toyota-bound hydrogen production equipment mass production
Reuters reported that Elliott holds a meaningful stake in Mitsui O.S.K. Lines and is working with the company on its midterm plan
Tokyo Electric Power Holdings jumped more than 14% intraday on capital-alliance reports and privatization speculation
Reiwa AH revised both its full-year earnings outlook and year-end dividend forecast upward
OpenWork announced an initial dividend policy and consolidated guidance, with the subsidiary deal adding another catalyst
tripla posted 73% operating-profit growth in the first quarter, helped by growth in booked facilities

watchlist.name: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines
watchlist.why_today: Reuters reported a meaningful Elliott stake and cooperation on the new midterm plan. With PBR around 0.67x in the story, the stock remains a capital-policy check name into the afternoon.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading near session highs after the lunch break
Does buying stay concentrated in MOL rather than the shipping group broadly
Does the late-March midterm-plan angle keep supporting the tape
falsifiers: Additional company color weakens the cooperation narrative
The activist angle fades into a one-day supply-demand trade
watchlist.red_team: The move is news-led, and without concrete policy details profit-taking can return quickly in the afternoon.

watchlist.name: Chiyoda Corporation
watchlist.why_today: It jumped as much as 15% on reports tied to Toyota-related hydrogen production equipment for 2029 mass production. The policy and hydrogen-equipment overlap keeps the name high on the afternoon monitor list.
watchpoints: Can it hold roughly the 15% gain area after midday
Does the hydrogen theme spread beyond a one-stock reaction into related equipment names
Does the market extend the story from Toyota linkage to future order potential
falsifiers: Short-term money leaves once the headline is digested
Investors decide the commercialization timetable is too far out
watchlist.red_team: The theme is strong, but the monetization timeline is long, which raises the risk of front-loaded pricing.

watchlist.name: 令和アカウンティング・ホールディングス
watchlist.why_today: It raised both full-year earnings guidance and the year-end dividend outlook, then moved to a buy-side indication after lunch. The combination of numbers and shareholder return makes follow-through the key afternoon question.
watchpoints: Does it remain order-book driven when afternoon trading resumes
Does buying continue even after the first print relative to the size of the revision
Does the dividend revision help stabilize post-open trading
falsifiers: Heavy profit-taking hits immediately after the first print
Turnover does not persist because it is still a smaller-cap name
watchlist.red_team: The disclosure is strong, but thinner liquidity can still turn it into a one-burst move.

watchlist.name: Tokyo Electric Power Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It surged more than 14% on capital-alliance and privatization speculation. The monitoring priority is high, but conviction stays low because the setup still rests mainly on media reports.
watchpoints: Can it keep a strong bid after afternoon trading resumes
Do the reports get more specific or draw a company response
Does abnormal turnover stay elevated
falsifiers: The market pushes back on the lack of concrete details
The reports are corrected, softened or denied
watchlist.red_team: The core catalyst is still report-based, so price action can outrun confirmation very easily.

watchlist.name: Mitsubishi Materials
watchlist.why_today: It emerged as a central rare-earth and copper beneficiary on the U.S.-Japan joint-development report. The afternoon test is whether the theme keeps extending rather than fading as a morning-only burst.
watchpoints: Can it remain the lead vehicle within the related group
Does the policy-driven rare-earth and copper theme survive into the afternoon
Can it keep stronger relative action than the trading houses
falsifiers: Theme buying is exhausted in the morning session
Investors focus on the lack of concrete detail in the U.S.-Japan agreement
watchlist.red_team: The theme is powerful, but without visible demand or earnings timing the staying power can weaken.

sources.title: 三菱マテリアル－買い気配 レアアース・銅、日米共同開発 首脳会談で合意へ 同社など参加＝日経

watchlist.name: Mitsui & Co.
watchlist.why_today: It is a liquid secondary beneficiary of the rare-earth and copper joint-development story. As a large-cap trading house, it remains easy for institutions to rotate into during the afternoon.
watchpoints: Does money broaden from Mitsubishi Materials into the trading houses
Can it preserve relative strength while TOPIX stays firm
Does the overall resource complex keep attracting buyers
falsifiers: Capital stays concentrated in the direct theme leaders
The afternoon session reverts to index-led rather than theme-led trading
watchlist.red_team: Because this is a second-order beneficiary, it can be the first to fade if the theme loses urgency.

watchlist.name: OpenWork
watchlist.why_today: The stock is backed by an initial dividend policy, consolidated guidance and the subsidiary acquisition of a startup-focused recruiting firm. The afternoon issue is whether that multi-leg story keeps working.
watchpoints: Can it hold around the opening area after the initial jump
Does the market care more about M&A than the first dividend
Can it resist any broader weakness in growth names
falsifiers: Too many catalysts dilute the market's focus
watchlist.red_team: There are several positives at once, but the market may disagree on which one deserves the premium.

watchlist.name: tripla
watchlist.why_today: With first-quarter operating profit up 73% and facilities continuing to increase, the stock extended its rally to a fourth straight session. The afternoon test is whether buyers still defend pullbacks.
watchpoints: Can it absorb profit-taking even after four strong sessions
Is facility growth treated as evidence of durable expansion
Does capital remain in both travel-linked and growth-linked names
falsifiers: A sense of near-term exhaustion takes over after the rally
The stock cannot hold its morning highs
watchlist.red_team: Even with good numbers, extended momentum names often lose force quickly later in the day.

deep_dive.title: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines: Into the afternoon, the key issue is not the headline pop but what cooperation could mean

lines: A meaningful Elliott stake is more than a pure trading catalyst if it feeds into pressure for better capital efficiency in the midterm plan due later this month.
Among shipping names, MOL offers enough scale and liquidity for the theme to keep attracting capital during the afternoon.
Reuters reported on March 18 that Elliott holds a meaningful stake in Mitsui O.S.K. Lines and is working with the company on its new midterm plan.
The same report highlighted a PBR of 0.67x, making capital-policy upside part of the core setup rather than a side note.
Can the stock stay near session highs after the lunch break, and does buying remain more concentrated in MOL than in shipping peers.
Does the late-March midterm-plan angle keep attracting dip buyers with turnover still holding up.
If the nature of the cooperation remains too vague and the activist angle fades into a one-day speculation trade.
If weakness in shipping markets or the broader index overwhelms the stock-specific catalyst.
No specific shareholder-return or restructuring measures have been announced yet, so expectations can run ahead of facts.
In the afternoon, short-term momentum money can dominate and amplify volatility beyond what the news alone would justify.

no_trade_conditions: If follow-up information weakens the Elliott cooperation angle in Mitsui O.S.K. Lines
If the morning's theme leaders all lose momentum after lunch and turnover-backed follow-through disappears
If USD/JPY or crude reverse sharply enough to break the premise for resources, shipping and large-cap cyclicals
If company denials or factual corrections hit the report-driven names
